text:
"Shaykh Muhammad in alih al-'Uthaymin said:

"Certainly, respecting the scholars and the leaders is obligatory because religious legislation is upheld by doing so. The scholars are the carriers of the legislation and if they are not respected [as individuals], then neither will their sayings be respected. 

The leaders are the protectors of the legislations - meaning in regards to its application upon the Ummah, so if they are not respected, then this - i.e. implementation of the legislation, will be lost, and chaos will ensue. "

- Ibn al-'Uthaymin, Muhammad ibn alih, Wasayat wa at-Tawjiht li ulb al-Ilm (p.404)."

text:
"The misconception of excuse of ignorance in major shirk did not arise before the time of Ibn Taymiyyah. Those who argue for excusing ignorance in acts of shirk mostly cite his statements as their earliest basis.

It first surfaced during the era of Shaykh Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab ( ) in two forms:
1.From clear deviants, whom the Shaykh refuted in Mufd al-Mustafd.
2.From some sincere but confused individuals, including certain students in Diriyyah and al-Ahsa. The doubt subsided after.

It later re-emerged in the next generation during the time of Abd al-Rahman ibn Hasan, adopted by Dawud ibn Jirjis and Uthman ibn Mansur. Shaykh Abd al-Rahman countered it, supported by his son Abd al-Latif and Shaykh Aba Butayn through well-known writings.

In the third generation, it reappeared once more and was refuted by Shaykh Ishaq ibn Abd al-Rahman and Shaykh Ibn Sahman via fatawa and writings, with support from Shaykh Abd al-Latifs sons, Abdullah and Ibrahim.

(  Excerpt from           )"

The ghult and murjiah are two different sides of the same coin 

The extremists are like the mutaziliah when it comes to chain takfir and tabdee . They will rule your pet goldfish with riddah using some weird qiyaas because you never made takfir on Al-Adhir due to mawa3ni

Whereas the murjiah will see clear nullifiers yet turn a blind eye due to the lack of imaan and love for rulers . This is the fanaticism some of us have also when it comes to following the crowd or the person who speaks the loudest . Salafiyyah has been hijacked by the Jews in disguise .

Another thing Id like to point out is some individuals (no matter what side) having taassub to people or a certain someone . Know that this person will not grant you a ticket to the hereafter , no scholar is free from mistakes . But there is a difference between defending someone and defending their mistake . Brothers and sisters , we dont base our walaa off groups , no doubt wed find sincere ones who only wanted to seek shahadah and yearned to meet their lord in every faction . The blameworthy are to be blamed, and the defendants are to be given a defence .

text:
"Abu Bakr, without any iota of doubt.

It is a matter upon which the scholars have long agreed. Whenever a virtue is found that distinguishes one of the Companions, you will find that Abu Bakr possessed an even greater share of that very quality.

When patience and generosity are mentioned, the mind often turns to Uthman. Abu Bakr combined both virtues in a measure even more remarkable.

When the knowledge of Hadith is discussed, one immediately thinks of Abu Hurayrah. Abu Bakr knows unimaginably more Hadith than Abu Hurayrah

And when courage, firmness, and resolve in the face of criticism are praised, names such as Umar, Ali, Khalid, and others naturally arise. Wallai, even among these towering figures, Abu Bakr remains ahead of them all.

He is the finest of mankind after the Prophets.
May Allah be pleased with him and with all the noble Companions. Ameen"

text:
" AL MARKAZ AS SALAFI LEICESTERSHIRE FUNDRAISING APPEAL

Help Establish a Salafi Markaz (Centre) & Place of Worship in Leicester! 

 Donation Details at the bottom of the Tweet.

 Our Goal

Alhamdulillah, the Salafi dawah has a long history in the City of Leicester, with lessons and gatherings being held for over two decades. Many people in the community have greatly benefited from these durs & halaqt and the opportunity to study the Quran and Authentic Sunnah upon the understanding of the Sahabah & Salaf (righteous predecessors).

By the permission of Allah, a venue has now been earmarked where a dedicated Salafi Markaz and place of worship can be established.

We are now seeking the support of the community to help us secure and prepare this space so that it can serve as a centre for prayer, learning, and dawah.

 What This Markaz Will Be Used For?

The aim is to establish a simple and beneficial space that will be used for:

 Regular Islamic lessons and Study Circles
 Propagating the Quran and Authentic Sunnah
 Community Gatherings for beneficial knowledge & Tawun upon Birr & Taqwa
 A place where Muslims can learn their Religion correctly
 Working towards Establishing the 5 daily prayers in Congregation

This Markaz will, in sh Allh, serve as a place of worship and learning for the local community.

 Why Your Support Matters?

Establishing a place where people can gather to learn their religion and remember Allah brings ongoing benefit to the community in this life & the next.

The Messenger of Allah  said:
When a person dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who supplicates for him. (Sahih Muslim)

Helping establish a hub of ilm (knowledge) & ibdah (worship) can be a form of ongoing charity (Sadaqah Jriyah).

 How You Can Help

If you are able, you can support this effort by:

- Making a donation
- Sharing this fundraiser with others
- Making du that Allah allows this Markaz to be established

 HOW TO DONATE 

 GoFundMe:


 PayPal:


 Bank Transfer:
Name: Clear Guidance Ltd
Sort Code: 40-28-06
Account Number: 45387531

International Transfer
IBAN: GB91HBUK40280645387531
BIC/SWIFT: HBUKGB4B or HBUKGB4BXXX

May Allh reward everyone who supports this effort and place Barakah in it.",

text:
"  vertue toi  frquenter les pieux ! 

Cheikh Muqbil ibn Hadi al-Wadi'i   a dit : 

Je conseille  la femme vertueuse de svertuer  tenir compagnie aux femmes pieuses, ainsi elle gagnera en foi, en science et en clairvoyance, tout comme un homme devrait svertuer  tenir compagnie aux hommes pieux, car tenir compagnie aux mauvaises personnes peut ramener une personne en enfer, Allh   a dit sur les gens du paradis :

{Se tournant les uns vers les autres, ils s'interrogeront. L'un d'eux dira:  J'avais un compagnon qui me disait:  Es-tu de ceux qui croient que lorsque nous mourrons et que nous serons poussire et ossements, nous aurons  rendre des comptes?Voulez-vous l'observer d'en haut?  Il regardera alors et le verra au milieu de la Fournaise, Il lui dira:  Par Allah! Tu as bien failli causer ma perte!Sans la grce de mon Seigneur, j'aurais t de ceux qu'on expose au chtiment.} [As-Saffat  v.50-57]"

" A Mistake in Children's Tarbiyah

In many communities, there are children who grow up memorising Qur'an but without learning Iman.

This leads to a generation of Huffadh, who are ignorant of the correct Aqidah and the basics of Islam.

Jundub (radihaAllhu anhu) said, "We were with the Prophet as young boys, we learnt Iman before we learnt the Qur'an, then we learnt the Qur'an and we increased by it our Iman." [Ibn Majah]",

text:
"Surely, anyone who knows of the actions of Muhammad al-Eesa, and ascribes Islam to him  let alone believes him to be a senior scholar [of Islam]  has not smelt the scent of Islam. This goes without mentioning those that fund and promote his activities of heresy. (i.e., MBS and co.), and how that itself is enough proof for the Riddah of these enemies of Islam.

Two possible options for Fawzan:

1) He knows Muhammad al-Eesa is a Murtad, but cant say anything because hes afraid of those who inserted him in this position (i.e., MBS & co.). If he cant call out or fix such blatant Kufr, then his appointment is nothing more than a spectacle.

2) He doesnt know the reality of Muhammad al-Eesa, and thus affirms Islam for him. The problem with this, is that how can a Mufti of a land, be so ignorant of who and whats going on in that very land. Moreover, how can the leader of the committee of senior scholars, be so ignorant of the blatant and out in the open Zandaqa of the very members of that committee. Again, it just goes back to his appointment being nothing more than a spectacle.

A shame, Fawzan who is on the doorstep of his coffin, may Allah destroy him  and only Allah Knows when he will perish  cant even take a simple stand of truth, publicly, so that the truth is not clouded nor lost. What will he meet Allah with, to counter his decades worth of legitimizing the thrones of the Tawagheet who fight Islam and the Muslims?!

His current trajectory, and we ask Allah for Salmah, appears to follow in the footsteps of a [Temu] Balm, and be like how Allah Describes:

                             

And had We willed, We would surely have elevated him therewith but he clung to the earth and followed his own vain desire. So his parable is the parable of a dog: if you drive him away, he lolls his tongue out, or if you leave him alone, he (still) lolls his tongue out. Such is the parable of the people who reject Our Ayt (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). So relate the stories, perhaps they may reflect. [7:176]

What a terrible fate awaits the evil scholars that legitimize the Tawagheet. Verily, they themselves are Tawagheet,   ."

text:
"Do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Shaykh Khalid al-Fulayj (may Allah free him) said in his explanation of as Sunah of al Barbahari:

Islam cannot be firmly established except upon complete submission to the texts of the Book and the Sunnah. So do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Knowledge is not based on the minds and views of men; rather, it is with Allah and with His Messenger. So do not follow anything based on your desires, lest you deviate from the religion and exit Islam, then you would have no proof.

The Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) has clarified the Sunnah for his Ummah and made it clear to his companions. They are the Jamaat, and they are the great majority, and the great majority is the truth and its people. So whoever opposes the companions of Muhammad (salallahu alayhi wa salam) in anything of the Deen has disbelieved.

From this issue, an important principle is established: the texts of the Book and the Sunnah are not to be opposed by opinions, desires, tastes, spiritual feelings, or intellects. Rather, the stance of a Muslim regarding revelation is submission, belief, listening, obedience, following, and compliance, without disputing.

Then some ignorant people may come and narrate a hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari from Abu Hurairah in which the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) said:

If a fly falls into the vessel of one of you, let him immerse it, for on one of its wings is disease and on the other is cure.

Then the pretentious, the arrogant, the pseudo-intellectual, and the ignorant say: This contradicts reason. This is something impossible, how can an insect have disease in one wing and cure in the other?

We say: We believe in Allah, we believe in His Messenger (salallahu alayhi wa salam), we affirm, we hear, and we obey. Our Messenger does not speak from his own desire, rather, it is nothing but revelation revealed. So just as the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) informed us, we say: we hear and we obey.

As for you, by opposing the report of the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam):

If you know the report is authentic, then you have denied the Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) and committed disbelief by rejecting it.

And if you are ignorant, we teach you and establish the proof against you. If you persist upon your falsehood and misguidance, then you disbelieve in Allah the Most Great, because you have described the Messenger as a liar and claimed that his statement is not true.

But the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) does not speak from his own desire, rather, he speaks only revelation that is revealed.

So this is what is obligatory upon the Muslim: belief, listening, and obedience."

text:
Advice from Shaykh Abu Dujanah   (senior student of Imam Muqbil):

The Reprehensible Acts of Eid!
There are many violations during Eid that many people fall into. We will mention some of them so that we can be wary of them and avoid falling into them. These include:
1. Adorning oneself by shaving the beard: Know, my brother, that shaving the beard is forbidden (Haram) and a sin. The evidence for this is the hadith of Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches and let the beards grow.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5393).
And from Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches, and let the beards grow long; differ from the Magians.)  Narrated by Muslim (260).
Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this wording in the mentioned hadiths necessitates the obligation of growing and lengthening the beards, and the prohibition of shaving and cutting them, because the default principle regarding commands is obligation, and the default principle regarding prohibitions is prohibition, unless there is evidence indicating otherwise, and this is what is relied upon by the people of knowledge.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/372).
> 
2. Dyeing the hair black: Know, my brother, that it is not permissible to dye the hair black, and a severe threat has been reported for those who do so. From Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (There will be a people at the end of time who will dye their hair black like the crops of pigeons; they will not smell the fragrance of Paradise.)  Narrated by Ahmad (2470), and Abu Dawud (4212).
> (So this hadith is explicit regarding the prohibition of dyeing with black.)  Tuhfat Al-Ahwadhi (5/359).
> 
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this hadith necessitates the prohibition of dyeing gray hair black, and that it is from the major sins.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (11/123).
> 
3. Isbal (Lengthening the garment below the ankles): Isbal of the lower garment is one of the major sins because a severe threat is attached to it, and the one who practices Isbal has fallen into two prohibitions:
 * The First: He has exposed himself to punishment. From Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, who said: (Whatever of the lower garment is below the ankles is in the Fire.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5787).
 * The Second: He has exposed his garment to dirt and filth. A young man wearing a long garment entered upon Umar bin Al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, and he said to him: (O son of my brother, raise your garment, for it is cleaner for your garment, and more pious toward your Lord.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari in the story of the assassination of Umar (3700).
The Sunnah is for the lower garment and clothing of the Muslim to be at mid-calf. From Al-Alaa bin Abdul Rahman, from his father, that he said: I asked Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri about the lower garment, and he said: I will inform you with knowledge; I heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, say: (The lower garment of the believer is to the middle of his calves. There is no sin on him for what is between that and the ankles. Whatever is lower than that is in the Fire, whatever is lower than that is in the Fire. Allah will not look on the Day of Resurrection at the one who drags his lower garment out of arrogance.)  Narrated by Malik in Al-Muwatta (2/914).
So the Muslim must beware of Isbal.

text:
"Rojin davas, gayri slami sistemin nasl bir zulm ve hukuksuzluk rettiinin en ak kantdr.

 Beer yasalar vatandaa Allah korkusu ve iman bilinci kazandrmyor. Beer yasalara tabi insanlar yasa nnde mazbut, yasann ulaamayaca yerde olabildiince canileiyor.

 Yasa koyucular mutlak ilim sfatna sahip olmadndan su ve ceza arasnda denge kuramyor. ou 'ceza' caydrc olmak yle dursun, sua tevik ediyor.

 Yarglama srecinin uzunluu ve bu sreci reytinge kurban eden ahlaksz medya eliyle, her maduriyet magazinselleiyor. Gz yal anne ve babalar yllarca ayn acy her gn, her duruma tekrar tekrar yayor. Sistem adalet retmedii gibi adalet bekleyen madurlarn maduriyetini derinletiriyor.

 Sulu veya madur iktidar, sermaye veya sosyeteye yaknsa, sre ok hzl ve saylanlarn lehine iliyor. Aksi durumlarda sre uzuyor ve madurlarn aleyhine iliyor.

 kan karar ou zaman suun kendisinden daha fazla yaralayc oluyor. ocuu katledilen ebeveyn, vergileriyle maduru beslemek zorunda braklyor. Onlar ocuklarn kaybetmiken, sulular ya bir yarg paketi ya da kovid dzenlemesiyle dar kp hayatlarna devam ediyor. 

Sonu olarak; adliye nlerinde gz yal aresiz ebeveynler grmek istemiyorsak; adaletin Kzlay bakannn kzna ayr, Nizamettin Kabai'in kzna (Rojin) ayr ilemesini istemiyorsak, Allah'n nizamna, slam terbiyesine ve er'i yasalara dnmeliyiz."

text:
"This is my will and my final message. If these words reach you, know that Israel has succeeded in killing me and silencing my voice. First, peace be upon you and Allahs mercy and blessings.

Allah knows I gave every effort and all my strength to be a support and a voice for my people, ever since I opened my eyes to life in the alleys and streets of the Jabalia refugee camp. My hope was that Allah would extend my life so I could return with my family and loved ones to our original town of occupied Asqalan (Al-Majdal). But Allahs will came first, and His decree is final. I have lived through pain in all its details, tasted suffering and loss many times, yet I never once hesitated to convey the truth as it is, without distortion or falsificationso that Allah may bear witness against those who stayed silent, those who accepted our killing, those who choked our breath, and whose hearts were unmoved by the scattered remains of our children and women, doing nothing to stop the massacre that our people have faced for more than a year and a half.

I entrust you with Palestinethe jewel in the crown of the Muslim world, the heartbeat of every free person in this world. I entrust you with its people, with its wronged and innocent children who never had the time to dream or live in safety and peace. Their pure bodies were crushed under thousands of tons of Israeli bombs and missiles, torn apart and scattered across the walls.

I urge you not to let chains silence you, nor borders restrain you. Be bridges toward the liberation of the land and its people, until the sun of dignity and freedom rises over our stolen homeland. I entrust you to take care of my family. I entrust you with my beloved daughter Sham, the light of my eyes, whom I never got the chance to watch grow up as I had dreamed.

I entrust you with my dear son Salah, whom I had wished to support and accompany through life until he grew strong enough to carry my burden and continue the mission.

I entrust you with my beloved mother, whose blessed prayers brought me to where I am, whose supplications were my fortress and whose light guided my path. I pray that Allah grants her strength and rewards her on my behalf with the best of rewards.

I also entrust you with my lifelong companion, my beloved wife, Umm Salah (Bayan), from whom the war separated me for many long days and months. Yet she remained faithful to our bond, steadfast as the trunk of an olive tree that does not bendpatient, trusting in Allah, and carrying the responsibility in my absence with all her strength and faith.

I urge you to stand by them, to be their support after Allah Almighty. If I die, I die steadfast upon my principles. I testify before Allah that I am content with His decree, certain of meeting Him, and assured that what is with Allah is better and everlasting.

O Allah, accept me among the martyrs, forgive my past and future sins, and make my blood a light that illuminates the path of freedom for my people and my family. Forgive me if I have fallen short, and pray for me with mercy, for I kept my promise and never changed or betrayed it.

Do not forget Gaza And do not forget me in your sincere prayers for forgiveness and acceptance.

Anas Jamal Al-Sharif
06.04.2025

This is what our beloved Anas requested to be published upon his martyrdom.",
